title: Lattice Light Sheet Microscopy (LLSM) — Bessel-lattice 3D imaging
sub_domain: High-resolution 3D live imaging via optical lattice
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical domain literature for this specific principle.

E.description: Lattice Light Sheet Microscopy (LLSM) — Bessel-lattice 3D imaging: lattice illumination produces the measurement through a 4-node primitive DAG L.illumination.lattice -> K.psf.detection -> S.scan.axial -> int.temporal, with axial/z-step integration and Poisson signal noise + Gaussian read noise. Recovery is posed as a linear inverse problem that inverts the forward operator to estimate the scene-side 3D intensity. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~18; calibration-level mismatch (lattice_registration, dithering_phase_error, sample_induced_aberration) sets the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form imaging equation.
E.forward_model: y(r,z) = integral [lattice(r,z - z_k) * f(r,z) * PSF_det(r,z)] + n
E.world_state_x: Scene parameter of interest (density / complex field / lifetime / normals depending on spec).
E.observation_y: Measured intensity or photon counts from the imaging system.

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 3D intensity is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by the declared priors. Stability is moderately conditioned (kappa_eff ~= 18); lattice_registration dominates the stability cliff; dithering_phase_error and the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Poisson signal noise + gaussian read noise sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor, while TV / wavelet-sparsity / deep priors stabilise recovery at the ill-conditioned end of Omega.

C.solver_class: iterative maximum-likelihood (Richardson-Lucy class) [Richardson-Lucy-LLSM] | linear-operator + convex optimisation [Multi-view-LLSM] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [CARE-LLSM]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sqrt(sigma_read^2 + alpha * signal) / sqrt(photon_count). Mismatch-induced bias adds + C2 * |lattice_registration| + C3 * ||x - x_s||_1 (best s-sparse / low-rank approximation residual). Iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the MLE; epsilon_fn captures the monotone quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(H * W * Z * log(...)) per iteration; learned variants: O(H W Z * F_theta_cost) per forward pass
